data.revised_prompt:
Create a high-end editorial mood board for a programming language brand called CHIBA, themed 'Compiler Lab Notebook'. Vertical A4 paper-like layout. Background warm paper white #FAFAF7. Only one dominant accent color deep forest oscilloscope green #1F6B3E, with pure ink black #111111 for text. Flat design only: no gradients, no glow, no glassmorphism, no shadows, no 3D, no photo textures. Precise academic meets industrial precision, between Donald Knuth literate programming and Bell Labs technical reports. Strict 8px baseline grid implied, lots of white space, hairline strokes, tabular numbers.

Top header in small mono caps: 'CHIBA / BRAND v0.1 / COMPILER NOTEBOOK'.

Arrange contents in a clean grid of small specimens:
1) Color palette strip: five labeled thin rectangles showing paper white, ink black, oscilloscope green, dust red, and four neutral grays (total 8 swatches). Each swatch has its hex code in IBM Plex Mono caption beneath. Labels precise, tabular, minimal.
2) Typography specimen: the word 'CHIBA' set in a tight modern grotesk, weight 600, negative tracking. Below it, a small body paragraph specimen and a code snippet in IBM Plex Mono.
3) Double-rule border sample: outer 1px + inner 1px with 3px gap, labeled 'academic double rule'.
4) Small SVG-style line drawing of a type inference trace: hairline 1px green strokes on white, connecting code tokens 'let', 'x', '42', 'Int' to type nodes and arrows. Minimal schematic.
5) Section numbering samples: 'SECTION 01', 'SECTION 02' in mono.
6) Tiny dot matrix pattern strip at the bottom reading 'THINK FP RUN IN ASM'.

Overall composition: refined editorial board, museum-grade graphic design sheet, precise alignment, clean grid, technical report aesthetic, no ornament beyond the specimens, absolutely flat and crisp, highly legible typography, subtle paper-like warmth only from background color, no texture.
